app.admin.assessment.domainContext.conte: Provide basic details about this assessment.
app.admin.assessment.domainContext.descr: Describe the site conditions, species mix, and restoration or production goals.
Describe the training program, target audience, and learning objectives.
Describe the energy access situation, infrastructure being deployed, and target community.
Describe the waste stream, collection infrastructure, and target outcomes.
What land area and species are being assessed? What ecological or agricultural challenge does this address?
What training or educational program is being assessed? Who are the learners and what skills are targeted?
What solar energy challenge is being addressed? What scale of deployment is involved and who benefits?
What waste management challenge is being tracked? What materials and community are involved?
app.admin.assessment.domainContext.locat: e.g., Alto Paraíso, Goiás — Farm cooperative lot 3
e.g., Medellín, Colombia — Community learning center
e.g., Kigali, Rwanda — Sector 5 hub network
e.g., Dharavi, Mumbai — Mithi River corridor (2km stretch)
app.admin.assessment.domainContext.title: e.g., Cerrado Reforestation — Q1 Planting Assessment
e.g., Field Worker Training — Solar Maintenance Certification
e.g., Kigali Community Solar — Phase 2 Deployment
e.g., Dharavi Riverbank Cleanup — Monthly Impact Review

app.admin.assessment.shared.selectSdg: Select at least one SDG target
app.admin.assessment.shared.smartOutcome: Add at least one SMART outcome
app.admin.assessment.shared.targetPositi: Target must be a positive number
app.admin.assessment.strategyKernel.cyne: Chaotic
No cause-effect. Act first, novel practice required.
Clear
Known knowns. Best practices apply, cause-effect obvious.
Complex
Unknown unknowns. Safe-to-fail probes, emergent practice.
Complicated
Known unknowns. Expert analysis needed, multiple right answers.
Cynefin phase: {phase}
Classify the complexity of the operating environment.
Post-fire restoration with no baseline data and active erosion.
Emergency response training during active crisis with shifting needs.
Emergency solar deployment after natural disaster, no infrastructure baseline.
Waste crisis response with no existing infrastructure or baseline data.
Monoculture planting with established nursery stock and known survival rates.
Standardized curriculum with certified instructors and known pass rates.
Standard rooftop installation with proven equipment and trained installers.
Established collection routes with trained sorters and known buyer network.
Restoration of degraded land with unknown seed bank and variable rainfall.
Community-led learning with variable literacy and no prior baseline.
First off-grid deployment in region with unknown demand patterns.
New upcycling program with unknown community adoption patterns.
Multi-species agroforestry design requiring soil analysis and spacing models.
Custom training for multiple skill levels requiring needs assessment.
Grid-tied system requiring utility negotiations and custom inverter sizing.
Multi-stream sorting requiring material analysis and market research.
Cynefin Phase
app.admin.assessment.strategyKernel.desc: Provide context and goals for this assessment.
app.admin.assessment.strategyKernel.diag: Root-cause analysis of the challenge being addressed.
What land degradation, biodiversity loss, or agricultural challenge are you addressing?
What knowledge or skills gap exists? What are the consequences of not addressing it?
What energy access gap or infrastructure challenge are you addressing? What are the root causes?
What waste accumulation or management gap are you addressing? What are the root causes?
Describe the core challenge and its root causes...
e.g., Degraded pastureland from cattle overgrazing has reduced soil organic matter to <1%. Native species corridors are fragmented, limiting pollinator pathways...
e.g., Field operators lack standardized training on solar panel maintenance, leading to 35% system degradation in year 1. Knowledge transfer relies on informal peer learning...
e.g., Rural households in Sector 5 rely on diesel generators averaging 4 hours daily. High fuel cost ($12/week) limits productive electricity use and creates indoor health risks...
e.g., Unmanaged plastic waste accumulates along 2km of riverbank at 500kg/week. No formal collection infrastructure exists. Local informal recyclers recover <10% of recyclables...
app.admin.assessment.strategyKernel.sect: Define the challenge, outcomes, and complexity context for this assessment.
app.admin.assessment.strategyKernel.smar: e.g., Plant 200 native species seedlings across 5 hectares
e.g., Train 30 field operators to maintenance certification level
e.g., Generate 500 kWh/month from newly installed panels
e.g., Divert 2 tonnes of recyclable waste from landfill per month
Define measurable targets. Each outcome needs a description, metric, and target value.
app.admin.assessment.strategyKernel.titl: Summarise this assessment in a few words.

app.profile.ensChangeSupportDescription: Username changes need a hand from support right now. We can either help you release this name or look into recovering it if you've lost access.
app.profile.help.faq.badgesAndIdentity.a: Badges mark Green Goods milestones like joining early, documenting your first approved work, or supporting a garden. Your greengoods.eth name gives those badges a clearer identity.
app.profile.help.faq.badgesAndIdentity.q: How do badges and my Green Goods name connect?
app.profile.help.faq.dataStorage.answer: Drafts and media stay on this device until they upload. Approved work is stored as a public attestation so gardens can keep a durable record of impact.
app.profile.help.faq.duplicateHandling.a: Basic detection prevents obvious duplicates (for example, similar submissions within a short time window) to keep data clean.
app.profile.help.faq.gardensAndOperators: Gardens are the places or programs where impact happens. Gardeners document the work. Operators care for the garden record by reviewing submissions and approving the work that belongs there.
Choose the garden, pick the action you completed, add photos or notes, and submit. If your connection drops, Green Goods keeps the draft locally and sends it when you are back online.
How do I document garden work?
app.profile.help.faq.howToBringImpactOnc: You can bring impact onchain by creating a garden assessment and submitting it to the Green Goods EAS.
How can I bring impact onchain?
app.profile.help.faq.howToContact.answer: Telegram is the fastest path for help from the Green Goods community. You can also use the onboarding form if you want to share more context about your garden.
app.profile.help.faq.howToGetInvolved.an: Open Gardens, choose one that matches your work, and join as a gardener. After you join, the garden appears on your profile and you can start documenting work for it.
app.profile.help.faq.howToGetInvolved.qu: How do I join a garden?
app.profile.help.faq.howToLearnMore.answ: You can learn more about Green Goods by visiting our website, joining our Discord server, and following us on social media.
app.profile.help.faq.howToLearnMore.ques: How can I learn more about Green Goods?
app.profile.help.faq.offlineSupport.answ: Yes. Drafts stay on your device while you are offline, including photos and notes. When you reconnect, Green Goods uploads the saved work so you can keep moving in the field.
app.profile.help.faq.offlineSupport.ques: Can I use the app offline?
app.profile.help.faq.reviewAndApproval.a: An operator checks whether the work fits the garden and has enough evidence. Approved work becomes part of the garden record; rejected work can be revised or clarified.
app.profile.help.faq.reviewAndApproval.q: What happens after I submit work?
app.profile.help.faq.smartAccountAddress: Your profile shows a smart account address used for submissions and approvals. You can also link your regular wallet (EOA).
What is my smart account address?
app.profile.help.faq.whatIsEAS.answer: EAS is the public record Green Goods uses after work is approved. It creates a verifiable receipt of what was documented and who approved it, without making you manage the technical details.
app.profile.help.faq.whatIsEAS.question: What does attestation mean here?
app.profile.help.faq.whatIsGreenGoods.an: Green Goods helps communities document regenerative work, connect it to a garden, and turn approved evidence into a shared impact record.
app.profile.help.faq.whatIsImpact.answer: Impact refers to measurable positive environmental outcomes — such as planting trees, restoring soil, managing waste, or educating communities. Green Goods tracks these contributions across four domains: Solar, Agro, Edu, and Waste.
app.profile.help.faq.whoCanSubmitWork.an: Anyone who has joined a garden as a gardener can submit work. You do not need to be technical; you just need clear evidence of what happened.

public.fund.heroLede: Donate to a Garden's immediate Work, or Endow a Vault so yield supports the Garden over many seasons. Every contribution lands with a Garden, not a platform.
public.fund.heroTitle: A small gesture, <accent>growing</accent> over many seasons.
public.fund.title: Fund
public.gardenDetail.evidence.summary: Browse the Impact ledger to see Assessments and Attestations across Gardens.
public.gardenDetail.evidence.title: Evidence
public.gardenDetail.fund.description: Donate directly through the Cookie Jar or Endow the Vault — designed to preserve your deposit while yield supports the Garden.
public.gardenDetail.fund.title: Fund this Garden
public.gardenDetail.notFoundHelp: The link may be stale. Browse all Gardens to find what you're looking for.
public.gardenDetail.place.empty: Garden narrative will appear here as it is published.
public.gardenDetail.place.title: About this Garden
public.gardenDetail.work.summary: {count} {count, plural, one {Work attestation} other {Work attestations}} on-chain.
public.gardenDetail.work.title: Work
public.gardens.description: Explore regenerative gardens documenting impact on-chain
public.gardens.empty: Gardens will appear here as they come online.
public.gardens.heroLede: Each Garden is a real place where a community documents regenerative Work. The public record they build is meant to hold up under reading.
public.gardens.heroTitle: Explore the <accent>Gardens</accent> growing the public record.
public.gardens.title: Gardens
public.home.featured.empty: Featured Gardens will appear here as they come online.
public.home.featured.title: Tended places, openly recorded.
public.home.funding.body: Green Goods gives funders two protocol paths: direct support for verified Work, or a Vault position designed so yield can keep supporting a Garden over time.
public.home.funding.donateBody: Send direct support through a Garden's Cookie Jar for verified regenerative Work.
public.home.funding.endowBody: Deposit into a Garden Vault designed to preserve principal while yield supports the Garden.
public.home.funding.note: Donate and Endow support the Garden directly. They are not tax-deductible, charitable, or nonprofit-backed unless separately configured. Vaults also carry smart contract, token, yield, provider, and wallet recovery risk.
public.home.funding.title: Direct support today. Endowment support over time.
public.home.getInTouch.consent: By subscribing, you agree to receive seasonal Green Goods updates. Unsubscribe any time.
public.home.getInTouch.description: Quiet dispatches from the Gardens: what's planted, what's tended, what's ready to be funded. No urgency. No tracking. One opt-in.
public.home.getInTouch.error.internal_er: Something went wrong. Please try again or schedule a call below.
public.home.getInTouch.error.invalidEmai: Please enter a valid email address.
public.home.getInTouch.error.invalid_ema: Please enter a valid email address.
public.home.getInTouch.error.invalid_req: We couldn't process that request. Please review and try again.
public.home.getInTouch.error.luma_import: Subscription is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later or schedule a call.
public.home.getInTouch.error.network: We couldn't reach the server. Please try again.
public.home.getInTouch.error.origin_not_: Subscription isn't available from this page.
public.home.getInTouch.error.provider_un: Subscription is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later or schedule a call.
public.home.getInTouch.error.rate_limite: Too many requests. Please try again in a few minutes.
public.home.getInTouch.help: Single opt-in subscription. Email stays server-side.
public.home.getInTouch.scheduleIntro: Want to talk through a Garden, funding path, or partnership? Book a quiet half-hour with the team.
public.home.getInTouch.success.alreadySu: You're already on the list. Thanks for sticking with us.
public.home.getInTouch.success.inline: Thanks. Check your inbox to confirm.
public.home.getInTouch.success.subscribe: Thanks for subscribing. Updates land a few times a season.
public.home.getInTouch.success.title: We'll keep you posted
public.home.getInTouch.title: A letter, once a season.
public.home.hero.lede: Green Goods makes regenerative work easier to support, turning accessible contributions into a trusted public record of how land, water, and community grow healthier together.
public.home.hero.tagline: From good intentions to green outcomes
public.home.hero.title: From <accent>good</accent> intentions to <noBreak><accent>green</accent> outcomes</noBreak>.
public.home.install.description: Install the Green Goods app to log Work, capture evidence, and follow Gardens you support, even offline.
public.home.install.title: Bring the field with you
public.home.loop.assessBody: A Garden starts as a real community hub with members, roles, and a place-based brief, so every Work record has somewhere accountable to land.
public.home.loop.fundBody: Funders can Donate through Cookie Jars for direct support or Endow Garden Vaults designed so yield supports the Work over time.
public.home.loop.title: Four steps. Repeated, <line2>season after season</line2>
public.home.loop.verifyBody: Evaluators create Assessments that connect approved Work to evidence and impact across the Eight Forms of Capital.
public.home.loop.workBody: Gardeners submit Work from the field with media, details, and metadata. Operators review those submissions before they become part of the public record.
public.home.proof.assessmentsNote: Independent evaluator confirmations, anchored to a public reference.
public.home.proof.body: This isn't a dashboard. These are confirmed counts: Gardens tended, hands at work, entries logged, assessments held to. Public, verifiable, and small for now, on purpose.
public.home.proof.contributorsNote: Gardeners, operators, and evaluators with at least one confirmed entry.
public.home.proof.title: Quantifiable restoration.
public.home.proof.worksNote: Photos, soil cores, plantings, repairs, restorations, each timestamped.
public.impact.dialog.refs.media: {count, plural, one {# media item} other {# media items}}
public.impact.empty: Impact assessments will appear here once gardens publish them.
public.impact.evidence.dialog.noSummary: No summary published for this Assessment yet.
public.impact.evidence.empty: Assessment evidence will appear here as Gardens publish it.
public.impact.evidence.error: Evidence is temporarily unavailable. Please try again in a few minutes.
public.impact.evidence.partialData: Showing the most recent evidence we could load. More may be available across Gardens.
public.impact.evidence.sourceLimitReache: We're showing a capped slice for v1; deeper history will arrive as aggregation matures.
public.impact.evidence.title: Recent evidence
public.impact.filters.label: Filter evidence by record kind and domain
public.impact.heroLede: Green Goods turns documented regenerative Work into evidence the public can read. Assessments come first, then Work, then — when ready — an Impact Certificate.
public.impact.ledger.intro: Filter by record kind or domain. Each row links to the source — the full assessment, work entry, or certificate behind the line.
public.impact.ledger.title: Recent evidence across Gardens.
public.impact.pipeline.footnote: Most cycles produce an Assessment and Work. The Impact Certificate is the rare end-state, issued only when the source record will hold up to public reading.
public.impact.pipeline.intro: Each Garden moves through three stages of evidence — and starts again. The cycle is what turns a place's intentions into something the public can verify.
public.impact.pipeline.title: From plan to public proof, season after season.
public.impact.proof.certificatesNote: Not public yet — first issuance pending.
public.impact.proof.title: What the public record holds today.
public.impact.title: Impact
public.install.copyToast.message: Open the link in your recommended browser to install.
public.install.copyToast.title: Link copied
public.installDialog.description: Scan the code with your phone, then install the app from Safari or Chrome.
public.installDialog.message: Green Goods works best close to the garden: quick records, field photos, and offline-friendly work logs from your phone.
public.installDialog.mobileDescription: Use your browser's install controls. If this browser cannot install apps, open this page in Safari or Chrome first.
public.installDialog.mobileFallback: Open this page in your phone's recommended browser, then use the browser menu to add Green Goods to your home screen.
public.installDialog.mobileTitle: Install Green Goods on this phone
public.installDialog.qrLabel: QR code for the Green Goods app
public.installDialog.step1: Scan the QR code with your phone camera.
public.installDialog.step2: Open the site in Safari on iPhone or Chrome on Android.
public.installDialog.step3: Use Share, then Add to Home Screen on iPhone, or the browser menu, then Install app on Android.
public.installDialog.title: Bring Green Goods into the field
app.campaignCookieJar.create.title: Create cookie jar
app.campaignCookieJar.metadata.title: Update metadata
app.campaignCookieJar.sync.title: Sync allowlist
cockpit.community.cookies.createSubmitte: The wallet returned a submitted transaction without a final jar address. Once the transaction is executed, paste the created jar address to generate the public link and seed the sync form.
cockpit.community.cookies.deployerOnly: This surface is intended for deployer and ops wallets. Connect a deployer wallet to create jars, or the jar owner to sync an existing jar.
cockpit.community.cookies.description: Create one shared Cookie Jar for a campaign and allow selected garden operators to claim from it.
cockpit.community.cookies.dialogDescript: Create a campaign jar from the deployed factory. Jar ownership defaults to this wallet; production should use the ops Safe.
cockpit.community.cookies.extraPlacehold: Paste addresses separated by commas, spaces, or new lines
cockpit.community.cookies.factoryMissing: Cookie Jar factory discovery is not configured on this network yet.
cockpit.community.cookies.jarOwnerRequir: Connect the jar owner or ops Safe to grant, revoke, and update campaign metadata.
cockpit.community.cookies.syncDescriptio: Allowlists are snapshots. Recompute operators from the selected gardens, review the diff, then sync jar access and campaign metadata.
cockpit.community.cookies.title: Campaign cookie jars
cockpit.community.cookies.tokenDecimalsR: Token decimals could not be read. Check the ERC20 address and try again.
public.cookies.alreadyClaimed: This wallet has already taken its cookie from this jar.
public.cookies.browseDescription: Choose a campaign below. The claim and deposit tools open here, so the page stays easy to share.
public.cookies.claimExceedsBalance: The jar does not have enough crumbs for that claim.
public.cookies.connectHint: Connect your wallet to check the jar and take a cookie.
public.cookies.description: Shared jars hold campaign funds in one place. Operators from selected Gardens claim their share; supporters top up the jar for the next round.
public.cookies.emptyList: Campaign jars will appear here once the Green Goods team configures them.
public.cookies.invalidJar: "{jar}" is not a valid jar address.
public.cookies.loadFailed: This cookie jar could not be loaded. Check the link and try again.
public.cookies.missingJar: Open this page with a jar link, like /cookies?jar=0x...
public.cookies.notEligible: This jar is for garden operators on the campaign allowlist. Your wallet is not on the list yet.
public.cookies.registryLoadFailed: The campaign jar list could not be loaded. Direct jar links still work.
public.cookies.partialRead: Some cookie jar details could not be confirmed. The available state is shown below.
public.cookies.paused: This jar is paused for now.
public.cookies.ready: You are on the list. Take a cookie when you are ready.
public.cookies.title: Shared <accent>jars</accent> for campaign funds.
public.cookies.unknownCampaign: The "{campaign}" cookie jar is not configured yet. Use a direct jar link for now.
